title: CAU Imphal ORDINANCE 3 of 1995

Imphal, the 22nd September. 1995 No 3 of 1995 No. VC./CAL1.14(FSTABLIS)/94: Whereas the Central Agricultural University Ordinance No. I of 1995 made on tck 20th February, 1995 provides for the establishment of the College of Veterinary Sciences and Animal Husbandry at Selesih in the State of Mizoram under the Said University with total initial intake capacity of 22 students to the Bachelor of Veterinary Science and Animal Husbandry (B.V.Sc. and Ai 1.4 degree programme. including those to he admitted as per the nominations made by the Veterinary Council of : India Indian Council of Agricultural Research. And whereas the Government of India in the Depanrrient of Agricultural Research and Education Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICA.R) has approved the making of the first Ordinance regarding the admission to and enrolment of students to B. V.Sc.. • and A.H. degree course in the Central Agricultural University vide letter No.F.42-1/94- Edn. IV/TC-11 :g) dated 8.9.95. Now, theretbre, in exercise of the powers conferred by sub-section (2) read with clause (a) of sub-section (1) of section 27 of the Central Agricultural University Act, 1992 (40 of 1992), and with the previous approval of the Central Government, as aforesaid, 1, the Vice-Chancellor of the Central Agricultural Universit: 1Thphal, do hereby make this first Ordinance and notify that the seats for admission of 22 students to the 1st year B.V.Sc. and A.H. programmd in 'the Central Agricultural Univeisity shall stand allotted in the following rnar:ner to the States of the North-Eastern Region and the , - 5•02)". The selection of the candidates shall he on the b2SiS of marks obtained in the qualitVing examinations/test keeping in view the reservation- policies, if any. of the concerned State Government-central authorities The mInimum qualification for admission to B. A weightage of five per cent marks on aggregate shall be given to the candidates with at least three year of regular service in any Government organisation and to the Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes candidates.
